overrideJustification: The blocker is a legitimate contract tension, not a regression. VAL-DSH-010 enumerates the required Settings sidebar sections/rows (Device Profile, Group Profile, Rotate Share, Export & Backup, Profile Security) without forbidding extras. VAL-CROSS-008 requires a dashboard → /rotate-keyset click path with a button labelled 'Rotate' or 'Rotate Keyset'. Paper reference does not show any Rotate Keyset affordance anywhere on the dashboard surface (verified: rg 'Rotate|rotate' under igloo-paper/screens/dashboard/ returns only 'Rotate Share'). Placing a Rotate Keyset action row inside the sidebar's existing Rotate Share section is the minimum-viable resolution — it satisfies VAL-CROSS-008 without contradicting VAL-DSH-010's required row list. The existing DashboardSettingsSidebarFidelity.test.tsx still asserts all VAL-DSH-010 required sections/rows, and DashboardInteractions.test.tsx pins VAL-CROSS-008 via the sidebar Rotate Keyset click. Orchestrator applies override. The non-blocking 'metadata drift' observation is already being addressed feature-by-feature (6+ feature descriptions corrected mid-mission) and does not block sealing.

blockingIssues.description: Settings sidebar still includes a "Rotate Keyset" action row/button in src/screens/DashboardScreen/sidebar/SettingsSidebar.tsx:212, which violates the feature contract for sidebar scope.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a mission-authoring rule that each feature's description, expectedBehavior, and validation-contract IDs must be internally consistent before assignment; if they conflict, contract text is authoritative and the mismatch must be recorded in discoveredIssues.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Multiple dashboard-settings-modals-cross reviews reported feature metadata drift (sidebar scope, export modal content/close affordance wording, and recover-path phrasing) that conflicted with contract/Paper expectations.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: In ui-builder handoff requirements, explicitly require a discoveredIssues entry whenever feature metadata conflicts with validation-contract behavior, even when implementation follows the contract.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: dashboard-interactions review noted an unlogged mismatch between feature metadata and contract regarding Recover navigation surface.

rejectedObservations.observation: Treat each individual metadata wording drift as an independent guidance update.
